title: Terminals API
description: Surfboard offers a diverse range of hardware terminals along with the SoftPOS terminal that can be integrated with your existing ECRs and checkout systems. The Terminals API allows you to register and manage these terminals. For CheckoutPro integration, you can use the Terminals APIs to link the terminal to your CheckoutPro, check the linkage status, and delink the terminal from your CheckoutPro.

apis.heading: Register Device
apis.description: Registers a new terminal to store. For SDK-based workflows, this API call is unnecessary as SDK methods covers terminal registration. After the terminal is registered to a store under the merchant, it cannot be repurposed to be used by another merchant. However, you can use the Change Store API to transfer the terminal to another store under the same merchant.

reqParams.name: registrationIdentifier
reqParams.description: Usually, the registrationIdentifier is a 6-digit code found upon starting the terminal. For the SurfPad and Printer, you need to use the serial number from the back of the device.

reqParams.name: terminalName
reqParams.description: Assign a name for your terminal. An optional param to identify a terminal for the user.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the terminal registration.

resParams.name: data
resParams.description: Response data.

resParams.name: terminalId
resParams.description: Terminal ID of the registered terminal.

resParams.name: registrationStatus
resParams.description: Represents the current status of the terminal. The possible values are:

possibleValues.description: The terminal has been successfully registered.
The terminal is already registered.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the terminal registration.

apis.heading: Get Device Registration Code
apis.description: This API generates a registration code and link that can be used to register devices to the store. The registration code can be entered manually on the device, or the registration link can be used for automatic registration via a QR code.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request, indicated as either 'SUCCESS' or 'ERROR'.

resParams.name: data
resParams.description: Response data containing registration information.

resParams.name: registrationCode
resParams.description: 6-digit registration code that can be entered on the device for registration.

resParams.name: registrationLink
resParams.description: Deep link URL that can be used for automatic device registration.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the request.

apis.heading: Register Online Terminal
apis.description: Register a terminal with an online store to start accepting online payments using this API.

reqParams.name: onlineTerminalMode
reqParams.description: Online payments can be carried out using any of these following modes.

possibleValues.description: In this mode, the payment page and all associated configuration is managed by Surfboard. This is the simplest mode to integrate as all that is required as an integrating partner is to redirect the customer to the payment page. The payment page needs to be the top level frame in any given context.
With SelfHostedPage, Surfboard renders the fields in your page. This provides you with maximum customization as to render the layout any way you want.
Set this type if you want to accept Merchant Initiated Transactions (MIT).

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the terminal registration.

resParams.name: data
resParams.description: Response data.

resParams.name: terminalId
resParams.description: Terminal ID of the registered terminal.

resParams.name: publicKey
resParams.description: For SelfHostedPage terminals use this key to initialize your Online SDK.

resParams.name: registrationStatus
resParams.description: Represents the current status of the terminal. The possible values are:

possibleValues.description: The terminal has been successfully registered.
The terminal was previously registered but has now been removed from the system.
The registration was unsuccessful.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the terminal registration.

apis.heading: Delink Terminal from Store
apis.description: Delinks the terminal from its registered store. Use this API to remove a terminal from its registered store. You cannot use this delinked terminal for another merchant. However, if you want to use this terminal in another store created under the same merchant, you can use the Change Store API.

reqParams.name: terminal$id
reqParams.description: Terminal ID of the terminal that has to be delinked.

reqParams.name: storeId
reqParams.description: Store ID of the store from which the terminal needs to be delinked.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the terminal delinking.

apis.heading: Change Store
apis.description: Changes the store to which the terminal is registered. You can use this API to utilize the terminal in another store created under the same merchant. However, the Terminal ID remains unchanged.

reqParams.name: terminal$id
reqParams.description: Terminal ID of the terminal for which the store needs to be changed.

reqParams.name: storeId
reqParams.description: Store ID of the store to which the terminal needs to be registered.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the store change.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the store change.

apis.heading: Link Terminal
apis.description: Links the terminal to the CheckoutPro. You can use this API to link the terminal to a CheckoutPro integration to accept payments.

reqParams.name: terminal$id
reqParams.description: Terminal ID of the terminal that needs to be linked with CheckoutPro.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the linking request.

apis.heading: Delink Terminal
apis.description: Delinks the terminal from its linked CheckoutPro. Use this API to swap the terminal between different Checkouts or vice-versa.

reqParams.name: terminal$id
reqParams.description: Terminal ID of the terminal that needs to be delinked from CheckoutPro.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the delinking request.

apis.heading: Check Link Status
apis.description: Retrieves the linkage status between the terminal and CheckoutPro. Use this API to obtain the current link state between the terminal and CheckoutPro.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request.

resParams.name: data
resParams.description: Response data.

resParams.name: linkageStatus
resParams.description: Describes the linkage status of the terminal with CheckoutPro. i.e ‘LINKED’ | ‘NOT_LINKED’.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the delinking request.

apis.heading: Update Terminal Name
apis.description: Update terminal name of a registered terminal using this call.

reqParams.name: terminalName
reqParams.description: Assign a name for your terminal. An optional param to identify a terminal for the user.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the update request.

apis.heading: Move Terminal
apis.description: Use this API to reassign a hardware terminal to another merchant. This is limited to partners who handle their own storage and deal with terminals in bulk.

reqParams.name: targetMerchantId
reqParams.description: Target Merchant ID to which the terminal needs to be assigned to.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the move terminal request.

apis.heading: Set Terminal Config
apis.description: Sets specific configurations for an individual terminal. Use this API to configure settings and parameters for a particular terminal.

reqParams.name: serialNo
reqParams.description: Serial number of the physical terminal.

reqParams.name: wifiSsid
reqParams.description: Name of the WiFi network.

reqParams.name: wifiPassword
reqParams.description: Password for the WiFi network.

reqParams.name: autoSleep
reqParams.description: Auto sleep time for the terminal in seconds. Terminal will sleep if no transactions are received in this time period. Only applies if the terminal is running on battery.

reqParams.name: preferredRestartTime
reqParams.description: Scheduled restart time for the terminal in HH:MM format. The default is 02:00am according to the device's timezone. It has a 1 hour restart window. For example, if set to 02:00am, restart could occur anytime between 02:00am and 02:59am. It is essential for the device to restart at least once every 24 hours. This might be particularly important for businesses that operate beyond the default restart time.

reqParams.name: preferredNetwork
reqParams.description: Preferred network for the terminal. It can be either 'WIFI' or 'GSM'.

reqParams.name: alwaysShowMinorUnits
reqParams.description: Determines how the amount is displayed on the terminal. It can be either '0' or '1'. When set to '1', the terminal will always display the minor units, even for whole numbers (e.g., 29 will be displayed as 29.00). When set to 0, the terminal will not include minor units for whole numbers.

reqParams.name: language
reqParams.description: Two letter ISO language code, in lowercase, representing the language to be displayed in the terminal. Can be 'en', 'fi', 'da' , 'se'.

reqParams.name: showStatusBar
reqParams.description: Determines whether the status bar is displayed on the terminal. If set 'TRUE', the terminal will display the status bar. This is applicable to SurfPad terminals.

reqParams.name: chipReadDelay
reqParams.description: Time delay in milliseconds before the card is read.

reqParams.name: showReceipt
reqParams.description: Set this to determine how receipts are showcased on the terminal.

reqParams.name: preferredGsmOperator
reqParams.description: The preferred GSM operator identified by a numeric code.

reqParams.name: openPosOnReboot
reqParams.description: Indicates whether the POS system should open automatically after reboot. Possible values are 'enabled' or 'disabled'.

reqParams.name: enableRefundLock
reqParams.description: Determines if the refund lock screen feature is enabled on the device. This is only available for android terminals.

reqParams.name: refundScreenPin
reqParams.description: The PIN code used to authorize refunds on the screen. This is only available for android terminals.

reqParams.name: screenTimeout
reqParams.description: The duration (in milliseconds) before the screen times out due to inactivity.

reqParams.name: autoSleepInterval
reqParams.description: The interval (in milliseconds) before the device automatically goes to sleep.

reqParams.name: receiptScreenTimeout
reqParams.description: Timeout of the receipt screen on hardware terminals.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the request.

apis.heading: Fetch Terminal Config
apis.description: Use this API to fetch all currently active configurations for the terminal.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request.

resParams.name: data
resParams.description: Response data.

resParams.name: wifiSsid
resParams.description: Name of the WiFi network.

resParams.name: wifiPassword
resParams.description: Password for the WiFi network.

resParams.name: autoSleep
resParams.description: Auto sleep time for the terminal in seconds. Terminal will sleep if no transactions are received in this time period. Only applies if the terminal is running on battery.

resParams.name: preferredRestartTime
resParams.description: Scheduled restart time for the terminal in HH:MM format. The default is 02:00am according to the device's timezone. It has a 1 hour restart window. For example, if set to 02:00am, restart could occur anytime between 02:00am and 02:59am. It is essential for the device to restart at least once every 24 hours. This might be particularly important for businesses that operate beyond the default restart time.

resParams.name: preferredNetwork
resParams.description: Preferred network for the terminal. It can be either 'WIFI' or 'GSM'.

possibleValues.description: Sets the preferred network of the terminal as WiFI.
Sets the preferred network of the terminal as GSM or mobile network.

resParams.name: alwaysShowMinorUnits
resParams.description: Determines how the amount is displayed on the terminal. It can be either '0' or '1'. When set to '1', the terminal will always display the minor units, even for whole numbers (e.g., 29 will be displayed as 29.00). When set to 0, the terminal will not include minor units for whole numbers.

resParams.name: language
resParams.description: Two letter ISO language code, in lowercase, representing the language to be displayed in the terminal. Can be 'en', 'fi', 'da' , 'se'.

resParams.name: showStatusBar
resParams.description: Determines whether the status bar is displayed on the terminal. If set 'TRUE', the terminal will display the status bar. This is applicable to SurfPad terminals.

resParams.name: chipReadDelay
resParams.description: Time delay in milliseconds before the card is read.

resParams.name: showReceipt
resParams.description: Set this to determine how receipts are showcased on the terminal.

resParams.name: preferredGsmOperator
resParams.description: The preferred GSM operator identified by a numeric code.

resParams.name: openPosOnReboot
resParams.description: Indicates whether the POS system should open automatically after reboot. Possible values are 'enabled' or 'disabled'.

resParams.name: enableRefundLock
resParams.description: Determines if the refund lock screen feature is enabled on the device. This is only available for android terminals.

resParams.name: screenTimeout
resParams.description: The duration (in milliseconds) before the screen times out due to inactivity.

resParams.name: autoSleepInterval
resParams.description: The interval (in milliseconds) before the device automatically goes to sleep.

resParams.name: receiptScreenTimeout
resParams.description: Timeout of the receipt screen on hardware terminals.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the request.

response.response:
{
	"status": "SUCCESS",
	"data": {
		"preferredRestartTime": "02:00",
		"preferredNetwork": "WIFI",
		"wifiSsid": "surfboard",
		"wifiPassword": "surfboa",
		"language": "da",
		"autoSleep": 400,
		"alwaysShowMinorUnits": 1,
		"showStatusBar": false,
		"showReceipt": true,
		"chipReadDelay": 500,
		"openPosOnReboot": "enabled",
		"enableRefundLock": true,
		"screenTimeout": 3000000,
		"preferredGsmOperator": 2
	},
	"message": "Terminal config queried successfully"
}

apis.heading: Fetch Terminal by ID
apis.description: Retrieves information about a specific terminal. Use this API to get information regarding a particular terminal registered under a merchant.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request.

resParams.name: data
resParams.description: Response data.

resParams.name: terminalId
resParams.description: Terminal ID of the terminal.

resParams.name: terminalType
resParams.description: Describes the type of the terminal.

possibleValues.description: Hardware terminal with a physical keypad.
Android terminal with custom payment apps, digital receipts etc.
Android terminal with a built-in printer.
Complete checkout solution with a customer facing screen.
Software checkout solution for NFC-enabled Android devices to accept contactless card payments.
SoftPOS solution on COTS devices.
In this mode, the payment page and all associated configuration is managed by Surfboard.
With SelfHostedPage, Surfboard renders the fields in your page.
Set this type if you want to accept Merchant Initiated Transactions (MIT).

resParams.name: softwareVersion
resParams.description: Software version of the terminal.

resParams.name: osType
resParams.description: Describes the type of Operating System(OS) running on the terminal.

possibleValues.description: Android operating system.
Apple's operating system for iPhones.
Surfboard firmware.

resParams.name: osVersion
resParams.description: Operating System(OS) version of the terminal.

resParams.name: deviceVendor
resParams.description: Device vendor of the terminal.

resParams.name: deviceModel
resParams.description: Device model of the terminal.

resParams.name: serialNo
resParams.description: Serial number of the terminal.

resParams.name: storeId
resParams.description: The Store ID of the store under which the terminal is registered.

resParams.name: linkedTerminalId
resParams.description: The Terminal ID of another terminal to which the current terminal is linked.

resParams.name: terminalStatus
resParams.description: Status of the terminal, it can be in one of these states: REGISTERED | ACTIVE | IN_ACTIVE | DE_REGISTERED.

resParams.name: terminalPaymentMethods
resParams.description: Payment methods supported by the terminal.

resParams.name: startDate
resParams.description: Timestamp at which the merchant was created.

resParams.name: terminalName
resParams.description: Name of the terminal used as a reference for users.

resParams.name: lastAliveAt
resParams.description: Time at which the terminal was last seen online.

resParams.name: isCharging
resParams.description: A boolean value that denotes if the terminal is charging or not.

resParams.name: batteryPercentage
resParams.description: Battery percentage of the terminal.

resParams.name: powerSource
resParams.description: Current power source to the terminal.

possibleValues.description: Connected to an external power source.
Charging via battery source.

resParams.name: deviceNetwork
resParams.description: The current mode of communication.

possibleValues.description: The terminal is connected to WIFI.
The terminal is connected to GSM.

resParams.name: turnOnTime
resParams.description: Last boot up time of terminal.

resParams.name: terminalPublicKey
resParams.description: Public key used while initializing SelfHostedPage Online Terminal (Online SDK)

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the request.

apis.heading: Fetch APN List
apis.description: Retrieves the list of APN's of the terminal. The terminal has to be active for a successful read and the response will be received in exactly 10 seconds.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request.

resParams.name: data
resParams.description: Response data.

resParams.name: apns
resParams.description: An array of strings depicting all APN's of the terminal.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the request.

apis.heading: Get Interapp Code
apis.description: Use this API to generate registration code used for interapp registration.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request.

resParams.name: data
resParams.description: Response data.

resParams.name: registrationCode
resParams.description: Returns the registration code for interapp. This code is valid for 120 seconds.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the request.

apis.heading: Get Interapp Details
apis.description: Make use of this API to fetch the current status of the interapp registration.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request.

resParams.name: registrationStatus
resParams.description: Denotes the registration status of the terminal.

possibleValues.description: Interapp registration successful.
Terminal not registered yet.

resParams.name: terminalId
resParams.description: Terminal ID of the registered terminal.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the request.
